mechanism_narrative: A positive trend in real activity inferred from rising retail sales signals improving near-term revenue expectations for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS was 390422.0 at the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating household income and recession risk, causing equity investors to reduce expected earnings and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE was 9.1 at the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains low or falling, markets price higher future tightening and lower real purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds its prior 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS is below its 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS was 0.07 at the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPI acceleration and low FEDFUNDS condition has nonnegative mean 60-day excess returns or does not underperform non-triggered periods in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in housing starts signals weakening household formation, construction demand, and credit-sensitive spending, reducing expected cyclical earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST was 629.0 at the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdown triggers are followed by higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered periods or by positive returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates improving capacity utilization and goods-sector earnings momentum, increasing cyclical profit expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive after being negative in the prior month
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO was 93.07 at the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO inflection triggers have a negative average 5-day S&P 500 return or a hit rate below 50% in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth and a falling unemployment rate confirm that labor income is expanding without relying on one noisy labor measure, raising consumption and earnings expectations and producing positive 20-day equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases over 3 months and UNRATE declines over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest available PAYEMS was 131190.0 at the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-up unemployment-down signal does not outperform single-series PAYEMS momentum or has a 20-day forward return hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate breakout signals an active tightening cycle that raises discount rates and slows credit creation, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 24-month maximum
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS breakout triggers are followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if triggered returns are not lower than unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales accelerate while unemployment remains elevated, the market prices a post-shock demand recovery with operating leverage to revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE is above 8%
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE was 9.1 at the decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if high-unemployment retail-sales acceleration episodes do not produce higher 20-day returns than other high-unemployment episodes or have a negative mean forward return.
